title: Hieracium neocoriaceum
taxonomy: Hieracium neocoriaceum is classified as a species of flowering plant in the dandelion tribe within the family Asteraceae. It belongs to the genus Hieracium, which comprises over 300 species of hawkweeds. The exact classification of Hieracium neocoriaceum may vary depending on the source, but it is generally placed within the family Asteraceae, order Asterales, and class Magnoliopsida. This classification reflects the plant's evolutionary relationships and shared characteristics with other members of the dandelion tribe. Hieracium neocoriaceum's classification highlights its unique position within the Asteraceae family.

intro: Hieracium neocoriaceum is a perennial plant species in the dandelion family (Asteraceae) native to the mountainous regions of Europe and Asia. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities, which can help alleviate various health issues such as digestive problems, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Hieracium neocoriaceum include flavonoids, sesquiterpenes, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. Traditionally, the plant is used to make herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often consumed to promote digestive health and alleviate symptoms of various health conditions.
